news.headline: Abivax strengthens commercial leadership team ahead of planned US NDA filing
news.summary: Abivax named four commercial leaders, including James Berger (ex-Amgen TEZSPIRE, ex-Takeda ENTYVIO) as Global Head of Marketing, spanning marketing, patient services, sales and market access, as it builds out US commercial infrastructure for obefazimod ahead of the planned Q4 2026 NDA submission.

news.headline: Abivax re-engages Centerview amid renewed takeover speculation
news.summary: French financial newsletter La Lettre reported that Abivax has re-engaged investment bank Centerview as speculation about a possible takeover picked up again following the company's strong ABTECT maintenance data and $920 million capital raise. Analysts have floated valuations for a deal as high as roughly $23 billion, though no formal offer has been confirmed.

news.headline: Abivax says $920M cash haul lets it prepare for solo U.S. launch instead of relying on a buyer
news.summary: CEO Marc de Garidel told CNBC that the newly closed $920 million raise extends cash into late 2029 and lets Abivax build U.S. commercial infrastructure for obefazimod rather than depend on a Big Pharma partner, tempering months of takeover speculation. He also disclosed a pre-NDA meeting with the FDA scheduled for July 30, 2026 ahead of the planned Q4 2026 NDA submission.

news.headline: Abivax closes upsized $920 million public offering of ADSs
news.summary: Abivax closed its underwritten offering of 7,360,000 ADSs at $125 each for gross proceeds of about $920 million (roughly $874 million net), after underwriters fully exercised their over-allotment option. Proceeds are earmarked to fund U.S. commercialization readiness for obefazimod and extend the cash runway into Q4 2029.

news.headline: Jefferies upgrades Abivax to Buy, lifts price target to $158, as Nasdaq listing begins
news.summary: Jefferies upgraded Abivax to Buy from Hold, raising its price target to $158 from $108, reversing its earlier downgrade after the supplemental ABTECT maintenance safety data eased malignancy concerns. The upgrade came the same day Abivax's ADSs began trading on the Nasdaq Global Market alongside its upsized public offering.

news.headline: Abivax shares jump 36% as new ABTECT data ease cancer-signal fears; Jefferies reverses downgrade
news.summary: A supplemental "Part 2" analysis of the Phase 3 ABTECT maintenance trial showed the malignancy cases flagged on June 2 were in line with expected background rates for the ulcerative colitis patient population, sending shares up as much as 36% and prompting Jefferies to reverse its earlier downgrade back to Buy. The update materially eased safety concerns that had driven a sharp sell-off in early June.

catalysts.label: FDA pre-NDA meeting for obefazimod
catalysts.detail: Abivax is scheduled to meet with the FDA on July 30, 2026 to discuss the planned New Drug Application for obefazimod in moderately to severely active ulcerative colitis, a key gating step before the formal Q4 2026 NDA submission.

catalysts.label: US FDA NDA submission for obefazimod (ulcerative colitis)
catalysts.detail: Abivax intends to submit a New Drug Application to the FDA for obefazimod in ulcerative colitis in the fourth quarter of 2026, based on positive Phase 3 ABTECT induction and maintenance (44-week) results. The company has begun building out its commercial leadership team ahead of this filing.

catalysts.label: EMA MAA submission for obefazimod (ulcerative colitis)
catalysts.detail: Abivax plans to file a Marketing Authorization Application with the European Medicines Agency in the second half of 2026, supported by the same positive Phase 3 ABTECT induction and maintenance data package.

catalysts.label: H1 2026 financial results
catalysts.detail: Abivax is scheduled to report first-half 2026 financial results and provide a pipeline/regulatory update on this date.

catalysts.label: Renewed M&A speculation (Centerview re-engagement)
catalysts.detail: Reports that Abivax has re-engaged advisor Centerview amid renewed takeover interest keep speculation alive around a possible Big Pharma acquisition, though management (CEO Marc de Garidel: "the best defense for us is actually the offense") continues to emphasize a credible solo U.S. launch path and no formal offer has been confirmed.

catalysts.label: Phase 2b ENHANCE-CD induction data (Crohn's disease)
catalysts.detail: Topline 12-week induction results from the Phase 2b ENHANCE-CD trial of obefazimod in moderate-to-severely active Crohn's disease are expected in late 2026, expanding the drug's potential indication beyond ulcerative colitis.

catalysts.label: Potential FDA/EMA approval decisions for obefazimod
catalysts.detail: Assuming on-time NDA/MAA filings in Q4 2026 and H2 2026 respectively, regulatory approval decisions for obefazimod in ulcerative colitis would be expected in 2027 or later.

pulse: Abivax's rebound from the early-June ABTECT maintenance safety scare has solidified into a full commercial build-out: reassuring supplemental data on June 30 triggered a 36% share jump and a Jefferies upgrade reversal, followed by an upsized, oversubscribed $920 million ADS offering that closed July 6 and now funds operations into late 2029. CEO Marc de Garidel has used the strengthened balance sheet to argue Abivax can credibly pursue a solo U.S. commercial launch of obefazimod, hiring four commercial leaders on July 23 (including a marketing chief poached from Amgen/Takeda) even as renewed Centerview-linked takeover speculation on July 17 keeps M&A chatter alive. Near-term focus is on the July 30 FDA pre-NDA meeting, the planned Q4 2026 U.S. NDA and H2 2026 EU MAA filings for obefazimod in ulcerative colitis, and H1 2026 financial results due September 21.
